sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Elche is in decent form, unbeaten in their last 4 matches with 2 draws and 2 wins in recent games. Levante UD, on the other hand, has struggled, losing 3 of their last 5 matches with no wins in the current league season.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Levante UD has dominated recent head-to-head encounters, remaining unbeaten in their last 8 matches against Elche, including a 3-1 victory in their most recent meeting. Elche has failed to keep a clean sheet in the last 4 head-to-head games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Elche shows balanced stats with a mid-table position (12th) and solid defensive numbers (5th least goals conceded). Levante UD, sitting 17th, has struggled offensively and defensively, conceding more goals than scored.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Elche's home advantage and current form give them a slight edge, but Levante's historical dominance in head-to-head matches cannot be overlooked. Both teams tend to play low-scoring games recently, often under 2.5 goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Elche and Levante UD ended in a goalless draw, with neither side managing to break the deadlock. Elche dominated possession and controlled much of the game, particularly in terms of territorial advantage and entries into the final third. However, their inability to convert this dominance into clear-cut chances or goals is a concern. Levante UD, on the other hand, had limited time on the ball but managed to create a notable opportunity, though they failed to capitalize on it. Discipline issues also crept in for Levante, with a couple of bookings indicating some frustration in their ranks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Elche's attacking intent
halftimeSections.text: Elche showed a clear intent to control the game, spending significant periods in the opposition half. Their ability to win duels and dominate aerial battles provided a strong platform for attacks. Despite this, they struggled to create high-quality chances, with only a single shot on target in the first half. Players like Álvaro Rodriguez showed promise with attempts on goal, but the final touch was missing. If Elche can sharpen their finishing in the second half, they are well-positioned to take the lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Levante UD's defensive resilience
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD spent most of the first half on the back foot, with minimal possession and fewer entries into dangerous areas. Despite this, their defense held firm, limiting Elche to speculative efforts rather than clear chances. They also showed glimpses of danger on the counter, with one big chance created but ultimately missed. However, their inability to retain possession and win duels could prove costly if Elche ramps up the pressure in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Throughout the first half, Elche consistently maintained attacking momentum, with several sustained spells of pressure in the opposition half, particularly in the opening stages and towards the end of the half. Their dominance was evident as they dictated the tempo for long stretches. Levante UD, by contrast, had only brief moments of attacking intent, with a noticeable shift in momentum late in the half, though it quickly faded. Elche's persistent forward push suggests they are more likely to capitalize in the second half if they maintain this intensity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Elche, Álvaro Rodriguez stands out as a potential game-changer due to his involvement in dangerous areas during the first half. His ability to get shots away could be crucial if he finds the target. Pedro Bigas also impressed with his contributions in both defense and attack, offering a threat from set pieces. On Levante UD's side, Iván Romero is a player to watch after missing a big chance in the first half. His positioning and expected goal threat indicate he could be the one to unlock Elche's defense if given another opportunity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Elche will likely continue to dominate possession and push for a breakthrough, potentially introducing fresh legs in midfield or attack to increase their sharpness in the final third. Levante UD may opt for a more counter-attacking approach, looking to exploit any gaps left by Elche's forward push. Defensive solidity will be key for Levante, but they must also address their lack of ball retention to avoid being overwhelmed. The second half could see a more open game if Elche's persistence pays off or if Levante capitalizes on a quick break.
